headline: Raymond James (RJF) Stock Could Be 32% Cheap On Strong Q1 Growth
summary: After a strong 110.6% total return over the past 5 years, Raymond James Financial now faces a valuation tension. The Excess Returns intrinsic value estimate points to meaningful upside, while the market price and earnings multiples look closer to about right and the broader checks are only a mixed endorsement. Over 5 years, Raymond James Financial has returned 110.6%, which means recent investors are now weighing fresh upside potential against gains that are already substantial. Stronger...
